title: Investigate MCP server / plugin tooling for beads
description:
## What

Investigate whether MCP server, Claude Code plugin, or other tooling exists (or could be built) to make beads (`bd`) usage more efficient inside Claude Code sessions.

## Why

Beads is the daily driver for task tracking — every session uses `bd ready`, `bd create`, `bd close`, `bd dolt push/pull`, `bd search`, `bd remember`. Two pain points:

1. **Permission friction**: `bd create` calls with multi-line `--description` strings (especially with code fences, quotes, or shell metacharacters) frequently trigger Claude Code's permission prompts because the command shape doesn't match a static allow rule. The skill workaround (write description to `/tmp/bead-desc-N.md` then `--description="$(cat ...)"`) helps but adds steps. An MCP tool taking a structured `description` parameter would bypass the shell-quoting issue entirely.

2. **Context cost**: each `bd` invocation is a Bash call with full output (lists, status, etc.). An MCP tool returning structured JSON could surface only what's needed and cost fewer tokens per query.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Survey: does an upstream beads MCP server exist (search github.com/steveyegge/beads + community)?
- [ ] Survey: any Claude Code plugin / community packaging that wraps `bd`?
- [ ] If nothing exists: assess the build effort for a minimal `bd` MCP server covering `ready`, `create`, `update`, `close`, `show`, `search`, `dolt push/pull`. (`bd` already emits structured output via `--json` in many subcommands — likely a thin wrapper.)
- [ ] Decision: adopt existing tooling, build a thin local MCP server, or stay on Bash invocations.

## Notes

- The workflow context document already promotes "use beads for ALL task tracking" as a hard rule — friction here compounds across sessions.
- Multi-line `bd create` permission prompts surfaced again in the 2026-05-03 session during `/bd-import-github-issues` execution.

title: Rewrite /retrospective to output beads + cross-repo Issues, retire retros.md
description:
The personal-infra split planning (paul-context/decisions/2026-05-03-beads-per-repo-issues-as-inbox.md) shifted the work-tracking model: beads internal to each repo, GitHub Issues as the cross-repo / sandbox-agent inbox. /retrospective still uses the old flow (appending bullets to ~/.claude/retros.md), which is reachable only on the local machine and creates a file that nothing else reads.

Target behaviour:

1. Run the same per-dimension session analysis as today (Continue / Start / Stop, plus the diagnostic dimensions: approval friction, CI round-trips, errors, pivots, prompt clarity, tool gaps, missing tools, MCP opportunities, Claude addon opportunities, memory updates, beads hygiene, slash command opportunities, etc.).

2. For each *Start* / *Stop* / actionable item, decide where the action belongs:
   - Same repo as the current cwd: bd create --title=... --type=task --priority=N here
   - Different repo (e.g. retro happened while in dotfiles but the action is about agentic-coding-config): gh issue create --repo pmgledhill102/<target-repo> ... — picked up by /start-session there via /bd-import-github-issues
   - Genuinely cross-repo or unsorted: gh issue create --repo pmgledhill102/paul-context ...

3. *Continue* items (validated patterns) and other reflective text that are not actions: skip, or capture in a bead/Issue with type=task priority=4 (low) only if the team would benefit from preserving the framing. Most Continue items are inert recall and dont need a bead.

4. Stop appending to ~/.claude/retros.md. The file is dead. Delete on next dotfiles change (dotfiles cutover PR #203 already removes it as a tracked path; retros.md sat in ~/.claude/, not in source).

Open design questions to decide while implementing:

- Should /retrospective auto-create the beads/Issues, or should it propose them and require user confirmation per item? Lean: confirm — retros are reflective and easy to over-action.
- For the "different repo" path, identifying the right target repo is non-trivial. Heuristic: the action mentions a repo name (e.g. "add brew install git-filter-repo to dotfiles") -> route there. Otherwise prompt the user.
- Sandbox-agent reachability test: a sandbox running /retrospective should still work. The sandbox has gh auth and bd in the current repos workspace; both flows work over the network.

Acceptance:
- Modified ~/.claude/commands/retrospective.md (lives in agentic-coding-config/home/commands/retrospective.md) implementing the above flow
- Per-item beads/Issues created during a real session, captured cleanly and visible at the right inbox
- ~/.claude/retros.md no longer being written to
- Documented behaviour change in the slash command itself (the doc-comment block at the top)

Related:
- paul-context/decisions/2026-05-03-beads-per-repo-issues-as-inbox.md (the design rationale)
- Bead paul-context-oed (security-process workstream: filter /bd-import-github-issues to my own issues only) — should be in place BEFORE this rewrite ships, otherwise sandbox-agent issues raised against public repos could mix with external spam.
close_reason: Shipped 2026-05-03 in commit 74b2d2a. /retrospective now routes findings to beads + Issues + memory writes per the design in paul-context/decisions/2026-05-03-beads-per-repo-issues-as-inbox.md. retros.md flow retired.

title: /start-session: reduce context cost of gather output and brief
description:
## Problem

`/start-session` consumes more context than needed. The gather script emits full sectioned output (raw JSON for `main_ci`, full preflight checklists, etc.), and the session brief itself is then rendered on top. For a clean repo this is mostly wasted tokens — the model only needs the deltas.

Surfaced 2026-05-03: a clean-repo run dumped ~40+ lines of gather output (full CI run array as JSON, preflight template lines, multiple "no issues found" blocks) before producing the brief. On dirty repos it would be worse.

## Goals

- Reduce baseline context cost of running `/start-session` on a clean repo to a small handful of lines.
- Keep all signal: failures, dirty trees, in-progress work, unmigrated GH issues — these still surface verbatim.
- Don't sacrifice debuggability when something fails (gather still emits raw stderr on non-zero exits).

## Ideas to explore

- **Gather script**: emit compact one-line summaries by default (`main_ci: green (959f7f6)`, `bd_ready: 0 issues`); only dump full output for sections with non-zero exit or non-default content. Add a `--verbose` flag for debugging.
- **Session brief**: collapse "none" sections to a single `(nothing)` line instead of a labelled block. Drop the framing rules when the brief is empty-ish.
- **Preflight**: the current output is a generic Go-project checklist that doesn't apply here — investigate suppressing or replacing with a project-aware version.
- **CI section**: parse the JSON in-script and emit just `<workflow>: <conclusion> (<short-sha>)` per workflow. Don't pass raw JSON through to the model.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Clean-repo `/start-session` produces <15 lines total of model-visible gather output (currently ~40+).
- [ ] All "needs attention" signals (red CI, dirty tree, unmigrated issues, in-progress beads) still surface clearly.
- [ ] Failure modes still show raw error output for debugging.
- [ ] Brief layout remains scannable (the current layout is good — just trim the upstream noise).

## Source

User-flagged during 2026-05-03 session right after `/start-session` → `/bd-import-github-issues` chain.

title: File upstream bd issue: pre-commit framework + bd dolt push conflict
description:
Source: https://github.com/pmgledhill102/agentic-coding-config/issues/1

From retro: `paul-context/journal/2026-05-03-personal-infra-split.md`

## Problem

When a repo has both `.pre-commit-config.yaml` AND bd is initialised (`.beads/hooks/`), `bd dolt push` fails with `fatal: this operation must be run in a work tree`.

**Cause**: bd internally runs `git push --porcelain --force-with-lease=...` to push `refs/dolt/data`, which triggers the local pre-push hook. The dotfiles git-templates dispatch hook calls `run_precommit pre-push`, which invokes the pre-commit framework. pre-commit then runs `git diff --name-only` as part of its `staged_files_only` setup — and that fails because bd is operating in a non-worktree context (`refs/dolt/data`, etc.).

## Local workaround

```sh
GIT_CONFIG_PARAMETERS="'core.hooksPath=/dev/null'" \
  PATH="/tmp/bd-modernize-shim:$PATH" BEADS_HOOK_TIMEOUT=2 \
  bd dolt push origin
```

(See `paul-context/lessons/bd_dolt_push_pre_commit_conflict.md` for the full workaround details.)

## Why file upstream

- Anyone using bd in a repo with `.pre-commit-config.yaml` on macOS hits this. macOS doesn't ship `timeout` by default, so the bd hook's `BEADS_HOOK_TIMEOUT` escape hatch doesn't fire.
- Anyone with dotfiles-style git-templates dispatch hooks: the `run_precommit` dispatcher fires unconditionally if `.pre-commit-config.yaml` is present.

## Suggested fix at `steveyegge/beads`

- bd dolt internal `git push` should set `core.hooksPath=/dev/null` (or pass `--no-verify`) for its non-worktree git operations.
- Or: bd hooks should expose a sentinel env var (e.g. `BD_INTERNAL_GIT=1`) that hook dispatchers can check to skip themselves.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Issue filed at `github.com/steveyegge/beads`
- [ ] Issue references this Issue and `paul-context/lessons/bd_dolt_push_pre_commit_conflict.md` for the workaround details
- [ ] Workaround documented in this repo (or in dotfiles) as a fallback if upstream fix is delayed

---

*Note: this Issue replaces beads `agentic-coding-config-orm` which was created via `cd + bd create` from a different cwd — that path is now explicitly forbidden by the `/retrospective` slash command (see commit 71ecf57). The replacement keeps the architecture's `cross-repo → Issue` rule intact.*

---
Imported from GitHub Issue #1 on 2026-05-03 via /bd-import-github-issues.

title: /end-session: detect stale ~/.claude/commands files removed from source
description:
Source: https://github.com/pmgledhill102/agentic-coding-config/issues/2

From retro: `paul-context/journal/2026-05-03-personal-infra-split.md`

## Problem

chezmoi does not auto-remove files from the target when they are removed from source state (unless `exact = true` is set, which has its own destructive risk for user-added files). Result: when a slash command is renamed or deleted in agentic-coding-config, the OLD file lingers at `~/.claude/commands/` on every machine that ever applied a version where it was present.

**Concrete example surfaced 2026-05-03**: `~/.claude/commands/bd-migrate-embedded.md` is still present on the local machine even though that command was renamed to `bd-modernize` back in PR #142 (in dotfiles, before the personal-infra split). The file is operational dead-weight — Claude Code sees and lists it as a slash command.

## Proposed enhancement

Add a check to `/end-session` that diffs `ls ~/.claude/commands/` against `ls ~/.local/share/chezmoi/.../home/commands/` (or the chezmoi-resolved external path for the agentic-coding-config mount). Surface any extras as candidates for `rm`.

## Acceptance

- [ ] `/end-session` warns when `~/.claude/commands/` contains files not present in the source
- [ ] Optionally: same check for `~/.claude/bin/` (less common to rename, but same drift mechanism)
- [ ] The check is fast (just two `ls` + diff) so it can run unconditionally without slowing the session-end flow
- [ ] User can dismiss the warning per-session if the extras are intentional

**Priority**: P4 (backlog) — small annoyance, not breakage. `bd-migrate-embedded.md` is benign; just visual noise in the slash command list.

---

*Note: this Issue replaces bead `agentic-coding-config-fs1` which was created via `cd + bd create` from a different cwd — that path is now explicitly forbidden by the `/retrospective` slash command (see commit 71ecf57). The replacement keeps the architecture's `cross-repo → Issue` rule intact.*

---
Imported from GitHub Issue #2 on 2026-05-03 via /bd-import-github-issues.

title: security-process: filter /bd-import-github-issues to my own issues only
description:
Source: https://github.com/pmgledhill102/agentic-coding-config/issues/3

From retro: `paul-context/journal/2026-05-03-personal-infra-split.md`
Part of: `paul-context` security-process workstream

## What

Modify the `/bd-import-github-issues` skill (lives in `home/commands/bd-import-github-issues.md` in this repo) to only import GitHub Issues raised by `pmgledhill102` (or a known sandbox-agent identity if added later).

## Why

Highest-priority guardrail in the security-process workstream: makes external Issue spam **operationally inert** even with public repos having Issues left enabled. This is the soft-measure that lets the no-lockdown decision (`paul-context/decisions/2026-05-03-defer-issues-pr-lockdown.md`) work — we don't have to disable Issues on `dotfiles` or `agentic-coding-config` defensively because spam can't reach beads anyway.

The decision rationale was: external Issue spam on personal repos hasn't been realised yet, so don't pre-emptively lock down. But the cost of that decision is taken on by this filter — without it, an external troll could populate every `/start-session` import with junk.

## Acceptance

- [ ] `/bd-import-github-issues` filters Issues by author at the import step (filtered-out Issues stay open on GitHub but never enter beads)
- [ ] Allowlist is configurable — initial value `pmgledhill102`; structure ready for adding sandbox-bot accounts later (decision pending in `paul-context-4k8`)
- [ ] Skill documentation (in the `.md` slash command file) updated to reflect the filter behaviour
- [ ] Re-tested after the change against a public repo with at least one mock external Issue (gh issue create from a different account, or simulate via the structure)

## Related

- `paul-context-oed` (closed, replaced by this Issue) — this work was originally tracked as a bead in `paul-context` but the implementation lives in this repo, so per the cross-repo→Issue architecture rule it belongs here.
- `paul-context-4k8` — sandbox-agent identity decision, which determines what additional usernames the allowlist needs to accept.

---

*Note: this Issue replaces bead `paul-context-oed` which was created in the wrong repo (paul-context) for a single-repo implementation task. Same correction pattern as today's `agentic-coding-config#1` and `#2`.*

---
Imported from GitHub Issue #3 on 2026-05-03 via /bd-import-github-issues.

title: File upstream bd issue: pre-commit framework + bd dolt push conflict
description:
From retro: paul-context/journal/2026-05-03-personal-infra-split.md

When a repo has both .pre-commit-config.yaml AND bd is initialised (.beads/hooks/), bd dolt push fails with `fatal: this operation must be run in a work tree`. Cause: bd internally runs `git push --porcelain --force-with-lease=...` to push refs/dolt/data, which triggers the local pre-push hook. The dotfiles git-templates dispatch hook calls run_precommit pre-push, which invokes the pre-commit framework. pre-commit then runs `git diff --name-only` as part of its staged_files_only setup — and that fails because bd is operating in a non-worktree context (refs/dolt/data, etc.).

Local workaround captured as memory:
GIT_CONFIG_PARAMETERS=`'core.hooksPath=/dev/null'` PATH=/tmp/bd-modernize-shim:$PATH BEADS_HOOK_TIMEOUT=2 bd dolt push origin

Worth filing upstream because it affects:
- Anyone using bd in a repo with .pre-commit-config.yaml on macOS (without GNU coreutils, the timeout-based escape hatch in bd hooks does not fire)
- Anyone with the dotfiles-style git-templates dispatch hooks (the run_precommit dispatcher fires unconditionally if .pre-commit-config.yaml is present)

Suggested upstream fix at steveyegge/beads:
- bd dolt internal git push should set core.hooksPath=/dev/null (or pass --no-verify) for its non-worktree git operations
- Or: bd hooks should expose a sentinel env var (e.g. BD_INTERNAL_GIT=1) that hook dispatchers can check to skip

Acceptance:
- Issue filed at github.com/steveyegge/beads
- Issue references both this bead and the local memory feedback_bd_dolt_push_pre_commit_conflict.md for the workaround details
- Workaround documented in this repo (or in dotfiles) as a fallback if upstream fix is delayed
close_reason: Superseded by github.com/pmgledhill102/agentic-coding-config/issues/1. This bead was created via cd+bd-create from dotfiles cwd, which violated the /retrospective architecture rule (cross-repo findings should always be Issues, never direct beads via cd shortcut). The /retrospective slash command was tightened in 71ecf57 to forbid this explicitly. /start-session here will sweep Issue #1 back into a fresh bead via /bd-import-github-issues.

close_reason: Superseded by github.com/pmgledhill102/agentic-coding-config/issues/2. Same correction as agentic-coding-config-orm: the cd+bd-create path was wrong, Issues are the canonical cross-repo path. /start-session will sweep Issue #2 back in.
